mechanism_narrative: A 5-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in the supply side of the economy, leading to higher demand for equities and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 5-day moving average of the S&P 500 returns over the past 6 months (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher wage growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 20-day moving average of the S&P 500 returns over the past 2 years (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the housing market index below 1000 signals a decrease in housing demand, leading to lower interest rates and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 60-day moving average of the S&P 500 returns over the past 3 years (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in real disposable personal income signals an expansion in consumer demand, leading to higher equity prices and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 20-day moving average of the S&P 500 returns over the past 2 years (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the GDP signal an expansion in the supply side of the economy, leading to higher demand for equities and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 5-day moving average of the S&P 500 returns over the past 6 months (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment above 140,000 signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher wage growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 20-day moving average of the S&P 500 returns over the past 2 years (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the consumer price index below 230 signals a decrease in inflation expectations, leading to lower interest rates and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 60-day moving average of the S&P 500 returns over the past 3 years (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in real disposable personal income and a 2-month increase in payroll employment signals an expansion in consumer demand and labor market, leading to higher equity prices and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: pre-recessionary consumer demand and labor market
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the 20-day moving average of the S&P 500 returns over the past 2 years (t-test p < 0.05).
